scientific_scope.statement: DBTT is treated as a macroscopic response class that can emerge from more than one microscopic competition, not as evidence for one unique mechanism.
interpretation_rules: Separate initiation toughness from propagation resistance.
Do not interpret the raw median K_J over 200-500 um minus K_init as a material R-curve without a geometry/control subtraction.
Use retained MPZ population and pre-renewal K_shield to identify shielding-based mechanisms.
Use an intrinsic low-shielding control to identify first-passage and geometry-driven contributions.
Preserve nonmonotonic peak-type responses as a distinct response class rather than forcing them into the primary DBTT class.

dbtt_primary.label: Primary DBTT: sharp retained-zone onset
dbtt_primary.mechanism_summary: Brittle/low-retention behavior through the lower-temperature branch followed by a sharp high-temperature onset of retained MPZ shielding and a positive propagation contribution beyond the common 2-D trajectory.
dbtt_primary.paper_use: Run the bulk of production DBTT simulations with this option. Use it as the main example of a shielding-assisted DBTT.
dbtt_primary.validation_status: Completed 2-D tip-only runs at 300, 700, 900, and 1200 K to approximately 500 um. Current primary as of 2026-07-19.

peak_primary.label: Peak type: nonmonotonic competition
peak_primary.mechanism_summary: A nonmonotonic toughness response produced by competing intrinsic kinetics and transient/retained shielding. The intermediate-temperature response exceeds the high-temperature response.
peak_primary.paper_use: Preserve as a distinct peak class. Use it to show that the same Arrhenius framework can produce a toughness maximum rather than a monotonic DBTT.
peak_primary.validation_status: Completed 2-D tip-only runs at 300, 700, 900, and 1200 K to approximately 500 um. Peak response centered near the existing 900 K sample.

dbtt_broad_shielding.label: Broad DBTT: sustained shielding
dbtt_broad_shielding.mechanism_summary: A broad transition with substantial retained MPZ population and sustained shielding already active by 700 K. The macroscopic curve remains strongly influenced by intrinsic kinetics and the common 2-D trajectory.
dbtt_broad_shielding.paper_use: Use as a short example that a DBTT-like initiation trend can coexist with broad, persistent shielding without producing a large geometry-corrected R-curve increment.
dbtt_broad_shielding.validation_status: Completed 2-D tip-only runs at 300, 700, 900, and 1200 K to approximately 500 um after increasing the step ceiling to 25000.

dbtt_intrinsic_control.label: Intrinsic DBTT control: negligible shielding
dbtt_intrinsic_control.mechanism_summary: A strong temperature-dependent apparent toughness response with essentially no retained MPZ shielding. This option isolates intrinsic first-passage kinetics plus the evolving 2-D geometry/loading trajectory.
dbtt_intrinsic_control.paper_use: Use as the no-shielding control demonstrating that DBTT-like behavior and a large apparent raw K_J curve do not by themselves prove plastic shielding or a material R-curve.
dbtt_intrinsic_control.validation_status: Completed 2-D tip-only runs at 300, 700, 900, and 1200 K to approximately 500 um after increasing the step ceiling to 25000.

dbtt_moderate_shielding_reference.label: Moderate shielding reference
dbtt_moderate_shielding_reference.mechanism_summary: A smooth monotonic temperature response with moderate retained shielding over a broad temperature interval, but only a small propagation residual after the common 2-D trajectory is removed.
dbtt_moderate_shielding_reference.paper_use: Retain as an intermediate comparison between the sharp primary DBTT and the intrinsic no-shielding control. It is not a production priority.
dbtt_moderate_shielding_reference.validation_status: Completed 2-D tip-only runs at 300, 700, 900, and 1200 K to approximately 500 um.

weakT_primary.label: Weak-temperature reference
weakT_primary.mechanism_summary: Temperature-flat reference response used to identify the common 2-D geometry/loading trajectory and to separate temperature-dependent mechanisms from specimen-level curve shape.
weakT_primary.paper_use: Use as the weak-temperature comparison and as one geometry-normalization reference. Do not assume it is a universal geometry baseline when initiation level or event sequence differs strongly.
weakT_primary.validation_status: Completed 2-D tip-only runs at 300, 700, 900, and 1200 K to approximately 500 um.
